englishDefinition: This site/app is for my personal reviewer. It may contain errors, wrong: info, spelling, translation, definition. Use at your own risk
Type the word in hit the circle or enter to search.
For finnish verbs type: verb or 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6.
some words that contain additional information type: z at the end of the word ex. moodz
table: some info & example charting for the ff.

translation: 6 types: verb1, verb2, verb3, verb4, verb5, verb6
englishDefinition: xxx = verb sample, V = vowels, <green>PP</green> = personal pronoun.
1: present tense  *EAT* (something happening or will happen)
2: past tense *ATE* (something happened in the past)
3: past perfect tense *HAVE EATEN* (something that happened before another action in the past.)
4: past participle *FEED* (something was already done to someone/something)
finnishDefinition: <green>PP</green> = personal pronoun ending (added to verbs at the end)

englishDefinition: adjective changes (changes at the end of the words)

translation: verbs ending in 2 vowels (xxxVV) = remove last V. (he & they strong form, the rest weak form.)
englishDefinition: 2: xxx<red><strike>VV</strike></red> = xxx + (oa, ua, yä, ä:replace last letter with i), (ää:remove & add si), (aa:remove & add oi) eä to si + <green>PP</green>
4: xxxV<red><strike>V</strike></red> = xxxV + ttu/tty (if last V is a/ä → 'e')
2: xxx<red><strike>dä</strike></red> = x<red><strike>V</strike></red>V + i + <green>PP</green> (remove first vowel & add i)

translation: verbs ending in lla, nna, rra, sta
englishDefinition: 1: xxxl<red><strike>la</strike></red> = xxxl  + e + <green>PP</green> (remove last 2 letters & add e)
2: xxxn<red><strike>na</strike></red> = xxxn  + i + <green>PP</green> (remove last 2 letters if V:o,u,y=+i if V:a,e,i=change to 'i')
3: xxxr<red><strike>ra</strike></red> = xxxr  + ut/yt, plural neet (remove last 2 letter)

translation: verbs ending in VtV: ata/ätä, ota/ötä, ytä/yta, uta/utä + (some itä)
englishDefinition: 2: xxxV<red><strike>TV</strike></red> = xxxV + si + <green>PP</green> (remove last 2 letters & add si)
3: xxxV<red><strike>TV</strike></red> = xxxV + nut/nyt, plural = neet (remove last 2 letters)

translation: verbs ending in ita & some itä
englishDefinition: 2: xxxi<red><strike>ta</strike></red> = xxxi + <purple>tse</purple> : change 'e' to 'i' + <green>PP</green>   (remove last 2 letters & add <purple>tse</purple> & change e to i)
3: xxxi<red><strike>tä</strike></red> = xxxi + nut/nyt, plural = neet (remove last 2 letters)
2: xxxo<red><strike>ta</strike></red> = xxxa + <purple>ne</purple> change e to i + <green>PP</green> (remove last 2 letters & add <purple>ne</purple> & change e to i)
3: xxxy<red><strike>tä</strike></red> = xxxa + nut/nyt, plural = neet (remove last 2 letters)

englishDefinition: 1. R slept well, uninterruptedly through the whole night.
2. R walks with walker to dining room for lunch.
3. R talks incoherently; nurse did not understand what was going on.
5. R has not explained why he doesn't want to join others for outdoor activity.

3. Asukas puhui sekavasti; hoitaja ei ymmärtänyt, mistä oli kyse.
5. Asukas ei ole kertonut, miksi ei halua lähteä muiden kanssa ulkoilemaan.

englishDefinition: 1. Resident ate breakfast with a good appetite.
2. Resident drank very little fluid today.
3. Breakfast taste bad. R  told that shes not hungry. He drunk cofee and ate small bread.
4. Lunch taste good, drunk two glass of milk.
5. R is on puree diet. had difficulty of swallowing.
6. R wish to get chocolates. R Relatives have been notified
7. Solid food does not taste good today. Feels unwell. Drunk juice/had a juice.

finnishDefinition: 2. Asukas joi tänään hyvin vähän nestettä.
3. Asmupala mastui huonosti. Asukas kertoi, ettei ole nälkä. Joi kahvia, Söi vähän leipää
4. Lounas maistui hyvin, Joi kaksi lasia maitoa.
6. Asukas toivoi saavansa suklaata (toivoo, että saisi). Asiasta kerrottu omaisille.
7. Kiinteä ruoka ei maistu tänään. Kokee voivansa pahoin. Juonut mehukeittoa.

translation: (Psyykkinen toimintakyky) mental functioning, related to mood, alertness, sleep (mieliala, vireystila, uni)
englishDefinition: 1. Resident was in a good mood during the morning shift.
3. Resident was tired and wanted to rest after breakfast.
4. Resident was angry/aggressive/in a bad mood and did not allow nurse assistance with morning activities.
5. Resident was more alert today than yesterday and was already awake when the nurse entered the room.
6. Resident was restless and often walked in the hallway.
8. Resident felt anxious and wanted to return to the room.
9. Resident was upset about forgetting things.
10. Resident felt embarrassed receiving help from others.
11. Resident was nervous about going outside.
finnishDefinition: 1. Asukas oli aamuvuoron aikaan hyvällä tuulella.
3. Asukas oli väsynyt ja halusi mennä aamupalan jälkeen lepäämään.
4. Asukas oli vihainen/aggressiivinen/huonolla tuulella eikä antanut hoitajan auttaa aamutoimissa.
5. Asukas oli tänään virkeämpi kuin eilen ja oli jo hereillä hoitajan tullessa huoneeseen.
6. Asukas oli levoton ja käveli usein käytävällä.
8. Asukas koki ahdistusta ja halusi palata huoneeseen.
9. Asukasta harmitti, ettei muista asioita hyvin.

englishDefinition: 1. Resident was able to walk with a walker to the dining area after lunch.
2. Resident washed her face independently and dried her body with a towel.
3. Resident had no energy to walk to the dining area and was assisted with a wheelchair.
4. Resident went outside with the nurse.
5. Resident was able to put on socks independently.
6. Resident needed support when going to the bathroom.
7. Resident needed a walker while walking today.
8. Resident was cared for in bed.
9. Resident had a conflict with another resident and had difficulty accepting others' opinions.
10. Resident had challenges adapting to new schedules.
11. Resident did not remember the familiar nurse.
finnishDefinition: 1. Asukas jaksoi kävellä rollaattorin kanssa päiväsaliin lounaan jälkeen.
2. Asukas pesi itse kasvot ja kuivasi vartalonsa pyyhkeellä.
3. Asukas ei jaksanut kävellä päiväsaliin ja hänet avustettiin pyörätuolilla.
9. Asukkaalla oli ristiriitoja toisen asukkaan kanssa ja haasteita hyväksyä toisen mielipiteitä.
11. Asukas ei muistanut tutun hoitajan nimeä.

translation: ihon kunto / kudosten terveys = skin conditions
englishDefinition: 1. Applied lotion to resident after shower.
2. Resident's armpits are red; cream/talc powder applied.
3. Visible bruising observed on resident's leg during the morning.
6. Resident scratches skin during the night.
7. Resident's underarms and spaces between toes are sore.
9. Resident's leg wound is draining; reported to nurse.
10. Resident's leg wound reopened; reported to nurse.
finnishDefinition: 7. Asukkaan rinnanaluset ja varpaiden välit kipeät.
9. Asukkaan säären haava erittävä; kerrottu hoitajalle.
10. Asukkaan säären haava auennut uudestaan; kerrottu hoitajalle.

translation: asukkaan tavoite esimerkki, client wishes or goals
englishDefinition: 1. Hopes for pain to decrease even a little, so that she can function better in everyday life.
2. Hopes for pain to be tolerable, so that she can enjoy life.
3. Willing to try different treatments if they can help reduce the pain.
4. Hopes to learn to manage pain better.
1. Hopes to move freely without pain.
2. Hopes to return to her hobbies.
3. Wants to take care of herself independently.
1. Hopes pain will no longer control her life.
2. Hopes to sleep better and feel calm.
3. Wants to feel happier and more fulfilled.
1. Hopes to receive support and understanding from others.
2. Wants to be active in her own care.
3. Feels good and can handle daily tasks independently.
4. Often tired; moving around is difficult but can manage at home with assistive devices.
5. Feels good mentally but physically weakened; family support helps.
6. Feels lonely but tries to stick to daily routines.
7. Likes the treatment but feels restless at times and would prefer calm guidance.
finnishDefinition: 1. Toivoo, että kipu vähenee edes hieman, jotta pystyy toimimaan arjessa paremmin.
2. Toivoo, että kipu olisi siedettävällä tasolla, jotta voisi nauttia elämästä.
3. Valmis kokeilemaan erilaisia hoitomuotoja, jos ne auttavat kipua vähentämään.
1. Toivoo, että kipu ei enää hallitse elämää.
2. Toivoo nukkuvansa paremmin ja olevan levollinen.
3. Haluaa tuntea olonsa onnellisemmaksi ja tyytyväisemmäksi.
1. Toivoo saavansa tukea ja ymmärrystä muilta.
3. Vointi on hyvä; pystyy hoitamaan arjen askareet itsenäisesti.
4. Usein väsynyt; liikkuminen hankalaa, mutta selviytyy kotona apuvälineiden avulla.
5. Henkisesti voi hyvin, fyysisesti heikentynyt; perheen tuki auttaa.
6. Koen oloni yksinäiseksi, mutta pyrkii pitämään kiinni rutiineista.
7. Asiakas pitää hoidosta, mutta tuntee ajoittain levottomuutta ja kaipaa rauhallista ohjausta.

englishDefinition: 1. R chat with the nurse aboout her family and reminiced about her childhood.
2. R daughter visited. they had a coffee in their room. Daughter brought new pants for her mother
3. R didn't chat/talk much this morning. She was quit.
4. R son called and ask baout her mother and told he will come next week.
5. R joined the others in the next room.
7. The spouse ask about her partner by phone.
finnishDefinition: 1. Asukas jutteli hoitajan kanssa. Kertoi perheestään, ja muisteli lapsuuttaan.
2. Tytär kävi asukkaan luona. Joivat kahvit huoneessa. Tytär toi mukanaan uudet housut äidilleen.
3. Asukas ei jaksanut aamulla jutella paljon. Oli hiljainen.
4. Asukkaan/Maijan poika soitti ja kysyi äidin vointia. Kertoi tulevansa käymään ensi viikolla.

1. Px agitation decreased after implenting an 'action'.
2. Wandering behavior managed, no falls during night shift.
-5 sec rule: is you ask questio wait for them to answer (they need more time to analyse and answer the situation).
-one idea at atime: instruct 1 action & wait them to do it then follow up (step by step).
-Validate: ex. if Px want to go work but is already retired 30 years ago = explain the work place is close + divert
-Do NOT approach them from behind or side always on front, stay at eye level & kalma lang

assist but maintain independency, let them choose.
validate what they say, use simple words, give time to answer.

ANSWER it like the 1st time + distract them with task.
VALIDATE it! ask more about that person.
if Frustrated, galit or bad mood.
wag patulan, Dont argue or use logic.

Do not give long info about the address.
ORIENT briefly (sä ot sairaalassa, olen sun hoitaja. Olet turvassa täällä).

translation: Diabetes mellitus: a chronic metabolic disorder related to high blood sugar
englishDefinition: It happen when pancreas does not produce enough insulin or the body cannot use the insulin effectively.
-Fasting blood glucose: ≥ 126 mg/dL : 7.0 mmol/L. (atleast 8 hours fasting)
-Random blood glucose: ≥ 200 mg/dL wtih symptoms. (doesnt matter when last meal was.)
Hyperglycemia = blood sugar ≥ 200 mg/dL : gradual onset Tx:give insulin prn, good diet
Hypoglycemia = blood sugar ≤ 70 mg/dL : sudden onset Tx:consious=oral agents for 15 mins then recheck blood sugar. Unconcious=give IV 50% dextrose D50 or glucagon(IM. IV, subQ.)
table: None produced: require life lonng injections
fatigue after meal, irritable, leg aches, impaired vission
DOB, nausea, sweet smell breath, frequent thirst & unrinate
oral agents: 1-2dl juice, 4-8sugar cubes, 1-2 tbsp honey/raisins, medium size fruit

translation: chronic brain disorder that affects how a person thinks, feel & behave. (a break from reality)

°Negative Sx (remove behaviors): things that disappear from normal person personality.
-Flat affect(wallang pake), Avolition(losing ADL things), Social withdrawal(emo time)
°Cognitive Sx are issues wtih memory lost, focus & making decisions
-Environment (high stress, trauma or over use of drugs)

translation: Pakastaa, Pakastua, Kohmettua, Syväjäätyä, Jäätyä, Viilentää, Viiletä, Palella, Jäähdytellä, Kylmetä
englishDefinition: °Kohmettua - intransitive: to get stiff from cold.
°Jäätyä - intranstive: to freeze like turning into ice
°Viilentää - transitive: to cool x down (The fan cools the room)
°Viiletä - intransitive: to get cool (a pleasant cold like evening breeze on the summer)
°Palella - to feel cold. (sensation of being chilly) 'I am cold:Minua paleltaa
°Jäähdytellä - to cool down like in exercise after doing sports.

translation: Lämmittää, Lämmetä, Kuumentaa, Kuumeta, Sulattaa, Sulaa, Keittää, Kiehua
englishDefinition: °Lämmittää - transitive: to heat x. (I heat up the sauna.)
°Lämmetä - intransitive: to get warm. (Sauna is getting warm)
°Kuumentaa - transitive: to make x hot. (I'll make the sauna as hot as the sun)
°Kuumeta - intransitive: to get hot. (Sauna is getting hot)
°Sulattaa - transitive: to melt x. (I am melting butter)
°Sulaa - intransitive: to melt. (the snow is melting:Lumi sulaa )
°Keittää - transitive: to boil x. (I am boiling water)
°Laittaa - to place x to somewhere (General term: like for everyday/casual use)
°Esittää - to express x in certain manner. (Present/show: like ideas/performance)
°Asettaa - to set/position x. (Careful placement: like formal/precise)
°Panna - to set into a state/conditon. (like putting a warning signs) *DO NOT USE* this word coz it has linguistic warning label
Verb type 3: to maul or savage 'by teeth'
Verb type 3: to express sadness, to grieve over

translation: Noudattaa, Vahtia, Valvoa, Observoida, Havainnoida, Tarkkailla
englishDefinition: °Noudatta - observe in a way of obeying/following x (adhering to rules or laws).
°Vahtia - keeping an eye/to guard x. (protecting/preventing) ex. guard on a gate / mother watching todler at playground.
°Valvoa - stay awake/supervise. ex. I stayed awake all night/ teacher supervise exam.
°Observoida - lawn word to observe formally or scientifically mostly use in academic, scientific & professional contexts.
°Havainnoida - to observe/percieve like gathering data & noticing details
Verb type 3: to tell untruths, mag sinungaling, mag chizmiz.
Verb type 4: transitive, to become aware or or reallize x.

translation: Liikkua, Liikuttaa, Siirtyä, Siirtää, Kulkea, Muuttua, Muuttaa.
englishDefinition: Verb type 1-i *(aa ää) (ea eä) (ia iä) (ua yä oa oä)*
°Liikuttaa - transitive: to move x. (I move the table).
°Siirtyä - intransitive: to move/transfer from A to B. (I move this from here to there)
°Siirtää - transitive: to move/transfer like reschedule. (Can yuo move my shift?)
°Kulkea - intransitive: to move via travel/pass through. (Bus goes once an hour/tame pass quickly)
°Muuttaa - transitive: to move to a new place. (I'm moving to a new home/He changed the plan)

°Käristää - to slighly burn x. (usually use on foods if its nakset.)
°Kärtsätä - to sear/sizzle x, cooking x on a high heat. (like cooking a fried rice in a wok)
°Saavuttaa - to achieved like goal, age, speed, success.
°Yltää - to reach x like height/lenght. (same with 'Yletä')
°Päästä - to get to arrive at a physical location.
°Pukea - transitive: to dress someone. (damitan na kita)
°Pukeutua - intransitive: to dress onseself. (nagdadamit na siya)
°Pyrkiä - to aspire mostly use in career
°Aikoa - to intend or plan to do something
°Tavoitella - transitive + partitive: to pursue x like goal, title or victory.
°Tähdätä - to aim with precision (like a weapon).
°Editoida - to edit in digitally like video, audio or code.
°Muokata - all-purpose verb to edit, change, adjust.
°Juoda - intransitive: to drink x. (uminom ka ng milktea.)
°Juottaa - transitive: to give drink to someone. (painumin mo siya ng gatas.)
°Syödä - intransitive: to eat x. (kumakain siya ng chickenjoy)
°Syöttää - transitive: to feed someone. (pakainin mo sila ng balut.)
Verb type 2: to bring x inn.
Verb type 2: to bring x out.
°Voida - to be able to permision/potential to do x. (sige gurl tulungan kita.)
°Saada - asking permision. (pwede bang umuwi na ako?)
°Pystyä - to be able to with the capacity/strenght to do x. (di ko kayang buhatin yan boss.)

finnishDefinition: KYS/HUS/TAYS -yliopistolliset sairaalat (esim. HUS = Helsingin ja Uudenmaan sairaanhoitopiiri)

englishDefinition: °Kontata - to crawl on all fours (use of hands and knees: Baby does before walking, man imitating a dog walk.)
°Ryömiä - to belly crawl (use of arms & stomach: soldier crawl under the barbwire, snake crawl.)
